manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: petroleum, pooch, morale, guile, puck, enzyme, adultery, cavalry, inventor.
Your opponent's team words are: chimp, opponent, drifter, census, vial, courtroom, physicist, condom.
Distractor words are: brat, motorcade, assailant, slipper, ultimatum, hypocrite, broker.
The kill words are: checkup.

We need to understand the game. This is a Codenames-like game: two teams, each with a set of words (their "team words"). The goal is to have your partner (the guesser) guess all your team's words before the opponent guesses theirs. The game also includes opponent's words, neutral (distractor) words, and a kill word (instant loss if guessed). The clue giver gives a single-word clue and a list of target words that the clue is meant to indicate. The guesser tries to guess which words from the whole board (the combined set of words) are theirs based on the clue.

We must produce a clue (a single word) and a list of target words from our team's list that the clue relates to. The clue must be semantically related to the target words, cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

We need to give a clue that is semantically related to some of our team's words. The more words we can link, the better. However, the clue must be a single word, not in any of the lists, and not contain any of the words as substrings. Also, the clue must be safe: it shouldn't be too similar to opponent's or distractor words that could cause the guesser to accidentally pick them.

We need to choose a clue that will allow the guesser to guess the target words.

The goal is to have your partner (the guesser) guess all your team's words before the opponent guesses theirs. The clue giver gives a single-word clue and a list of target words that the clue is meant to indicate. The guesser tries to guess which words from the whole board (the combined set of words) are theirs based on the clue.

- **Courtroom** (opponent): avoid words like trial, case, judge, legal, etc.
- **Physicist** (opponent): avoid words too close to science: physics, energy, molecule, force, etc.
- **Condom** (opponent): avoid words like protection, latex, rubber, safe, etc.
- **Motorcade** (distractor): avoid words like drive, car, vehicle, parade, etc.
- **Hypocrite** (distractor): avoid words like fake, false, pretend, lie, deception, duplicity, two-faced, etc.

- **Brat** (distractor): avoid words like child, kid, annoying, etc.
- **Broker** (distractor): avoid words like trade, market, stock, sell, etc.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: petroleum, pooch, morale, guile, puck, enzyme, adultery, cavalry, inventor.
Your opponent's team words are: chimp, opponent, drifter, census, vial, courtroom, physicist, condom.
Distractor words are: brat, motorcade, assailant, slipper, ultimatum, hypocrite, broker.
The kill words are: checkup.

CLUE: loyal
